**Step 1:** Write the boundary-review block for the three new hook families. Save to
`docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5a-pr-notes.md`:

```md
## Boundary review

### session:* family (`@ax/session-inmemory`)

- Alternate impl: `@ax/session-postgres` in Week 7–9 — identical contract, pg-backed inbox with
  LISTEN/NOTIFY wakeup instead of in-memory waiters.
- Payload fields: `sessionId`, `token`, `cursor`, `workspaceRoot`, `entry.type`, `entry.payload`.
  No sqlite / pg vocabulary. `cursor` is a monotonic int — same in both backends. No field name
  leaks.
- Subscriber risk: these are all service hooks; no subscribers.
- Wire surface: none exposed via IPC directly. `session:claim-work` is reached transitively
  through `/session.next-message` in `@ax/ipc-protocol`, whose schema is owned by the IPC
  protocol package per design-doc "Schema location."

### sandbox:open-session (`@ax/sandbox-subprocess`)

- Alternate impl: `@ax/sandbox-k8s` in Week 7–9 — pod-per-session. `handle.kill()` SIGTERMs the
  pod; `socketPath` becomes a cluster-HTTP URL. Contract identical, field `socketPath` generic
  enough to carry either transport.
- Payload fields: `sessionId`, `workspaceRoot`, `runnerBinary`, `socketPath`. No docker /
  kubernetes vocabulary (no `imageRef`, `podName`, `namespace`).
- Subscriber risk: service hook, no subscribers.
- Wire surface: not exposed directly; callable only in-process.

### tool:register / tool:list (`@ax/tool-dispatcher`)

- Alternate impl: none plausible today — the catalog is in-memory host-state. If a future
  multi-replica host needs a shared catalog, it'd register `tool:list` from a
  postgres-backed plugin. The current shape allows that.
- Payload fields: `ToolDescriptor { name, description, inputSchema, executesIn }`. `executesIn`
  is a closed enum (`sandbox` | `host`) — not a backend handle. `inputSchema` is an opaque
  JSON-Schema object (same pattern as Week 4–6).
- Subscriber risk: service hooks, no subscribers.
- Wire surface: `tool.list` IPC action — schema in `@ax/ipc-protocol`.

### IPC protocol (`@ax/ipc-protocol`)

- Alternate impl: transport is swappable per sandbox plugin (unix socket vs cluster HTTP);
  protocol schemas are identical. See design doc §"IPC protocol".
- Payload fields: `messages`, `tools`, `call`, `cursor`, `version`, `delta`. `version` is a
  `WorkspaceVersion` branded opaque token. No git / pg / k8s vocabulary.
- Subscriber risk: the hooks fired on IPC receipt (`llm:pre-call`, `tool:pre-call`,
  `tool:post-call`, `chat:end`) already have subscribers from Week 4–6 — their payload shapes are
  unchanged, so no subscriber breakage.
- Wire surface: this IS the wire surface. Every action schema is versioned implicitly by the
  `@ax/ipc-protocol` package version; 6.5b extends it additively (streaming variants), so no
  break-the-wire changes this slice.
```

**Step 5:** Scan for any leftover `@ax/*` imports that aren't on the allowlist.

Run: `pnpm lint` (already done; belt-and-suspenders).

**Step 6:** Verify the acceptance-test path.

Run: `pnpm --filter @ax/cli test -- --reporter=verbose`
Expected: the library-mode E2E logs the subprocess PID assertion.

**Step 7:** Open the PR.

```bash
git push -u origin feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift
gh pr create --title "Week 6.5a — topology shift" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
- Moves the agent turn-loop from in-process (`packages/core/src/chat-loop.ts`) to a subprocess
  sandbox driven via HTTP+JSON IPC over a per-session unix socket.
- Adds `@ax/ipc-protocol`, `@ax/ipc-server`, `@ax/session-inmemory`, `@ax/chat-orchestrator`,
  `@ax/agent-runner-core`, `@ax/agent-native-runner`, `@ax/tool-bash-impl`, `@ax/tool-file-io-impl`.
- Extends `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` with `sandbox:open-session`; deletes `sandbox:spawn`.
- Refactors tool registration to `tool:register` / `tool:list` on `@ax/tool-dispatcher`.
- Week 4–6 chat behavior preserved; acceptance test asserts the loop runs in a child process.

## Security review — @ax/ipc-server
<paste block from `packages/ipc-server/SECURITY.md`>

## Security review — @ax/sandbox-subprocess session-lifetime extension
<paste block from `packages/sandbox-subprocess/SECURITY.md` (new section)>

## Boundary review
<paste from `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5a-pr-notes.md`>

## Test plan
- [ ] `pnpm build` green
- [ ] `pnpm test` green (including the new topology-shift acceptance assertion)
- [ ] `pnpm lint` green
- [ ] Manual: run the CLI against real Anthropic with `ax hi`, confirm the bash PPID is not the
      host PID (proves loop is subprocess-resident).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
EOF
)"
```

**Step 8:** Run the final `code-reviewer` subagent against the entire diff (per
subagent-driven-development skill). If the reviewer raises a gap that maps to a new invariant,
append it to the Invariants block above and push a follow-up commit.

---

## Execution notes for the subagent driver

- **One implementer subagent per task.** Give it the full text of the task plus the Invariants
  block plus a pointer to `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md`. Do NOT have
  it re-read the plan file; pass the text directly.
- **Two-stage review per task:** spec compliance first, THEN code quality. Don't skip the loop.
- **If a task uncovers a new invariant:** append `I{n+1}` to the Invariants block, note it in
  the next commit's message, and re-state it when dispatching subsequent subagents.
- **Sequential, not parallel.** Tasks share files at the `tsconfig.json` / `eslint.config.mjs` /
  `packages/core/src/index.ts` level; parallel implementers would collide.
- **Don't skip security-checklist re-runs.** Tasks 3 and 5 each include a SECURITY.md write step
  — that's the moment to re-invoke the `security-checklist` skill, not at PR-push time.

---

## Out of scope for 6.5a (reminders, don't drift)

- Streaming (`event.stream-chunk` handlers, `chat:stream-chunk` subscriber, CLI incremental
  rendering) — ships in 6.5b.
- Other LLM providers (`@ax/llm-openrouter`, `@ax/llm-proxy-anthropic-format`) — 6.5b / 6.5d.
- Alternative runners (`@ax/agent-pi-session-runner`, `@ax/agent-claude-sdk-runner`) — 6.5c / d.
- `@ax/workspace-git`, real workspace-commit subscribers — Week 7–9. The stub response in
  `/workspace.commit-notify` is intentional.
- `@ax/sandbox-k8s`, `@ax/session-postgres`, `@ax/eventbus-postgres` — Week 7–9.
- Host → sandbox push. The inbox handles everything we know of.
- Dynamic tool registration mid-session. Catalog is session-lifetime-immutable.
- Docker / apple-container sandbox. Subprocess is enough for dev; k8s is enough for prod.

If a reviewer requests any of these, push back with "that's the next slice" — the scope
boundaries are already stated in the design doc.

Plan saved to `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5a-topology-shift.md` (17 tasks, ~850 lines).

Key structural choices:
- **17 tasks, sequential** — too much shared surface (tsconfig refs, eslint config, core/index.ts, test-harness defaults) for parallel dispatch. Each task has TDD-shaped steps and a single commit.
- **Invariants block (I1–I12)** at the top folds in what the design doc already specified plus the two reviewer-friction patterns from prior slices (explicit runner-binary path, opaque crypto tokens never JWT). Room to append I13+ as review surfaces gaps.
- **Security-checklist output baked in** as SECURITY.md write steps (Task 3 for `@ax/ipc-server`, Task 5 for `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` session-lifetime extension) — not deferred to PR push.
- **`sandbox:spawn` deletion (Task 6) deliberately ordered after the tool refactor (Task 7)** so `verifyCalls` never breaks mid-slice. Called out inline.
- **Acceptance test (Task 15)** keeps every Week 4–6 assertion and adds one new one: `bash`'s PPID must not be the host PID — the topology-shift contract in one line of test code.
- **Task 16 boundary-review block** pre-drafted for the four new hook families, ready to lift into the PR description.

# Implementer Subagent Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching an implementer subagent.

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Implement Task N: [task name]"
  prompt: |
    You are implementing Task N: [task name]

    ## Task Description

    [FULL TEXT of task from plan - paste it here, don't make subagent read file]

    ## Context

    [Scene-setting: where this fits, dependencies, architectural context]

    ## Before You Begin

    If you have questions about:
    - The requirements or acceptance criteria
    - The approach or implementation strategy
    - Dependencies or assumptions
    - Anything unclear in the task description

    **Ask them now.** Raise any concerns before starting work.

    ## Your Job

    Once you're clear on requirements:
    1. Implement exactly what the task specifies
    2. Write tests (following TDD if task says to)
    3. Verify implementation works
    4. Commit your work
    5. Self-review (see below)
    6. Report back

    Work from: [directory]

    **While you work:** If you encounter something unexpected or unclear, **ask questions**.
    It's always OK to pause and clarify. Don't guess or make assumptions.

    ## Before Reporting Back: Self-Review

    Review your work with fresh eyes. Ask yourself:

    **Completeness:**
    - Did I fully implement everything in the spec?
    - Did I miss any requirements?
    - Are there edge cases I didn't handle?

    **Quality:**
    - Is this my best work?
    - Are names clear and accurate (match what things do, not how they work)?
    - Is the code clean and maintainable?

    **Discipline:**
    - Did I avoid overbuilding (YAGNI)?
    - Did I only build what was requested?
    - Did I follow existing patterns in the codebase?

    **Testing:**
    - Do tests actually verify behavior (not just mock behavior)?
    - Did I follow TDD if required?
    - Are tests comprehensive?

    If you find issues during self-review, fix them now before reporting.

    ## Report Format

    When done, report:
    - What you implemented
    - What you tested and test results
    - Files changed
    - Self-review findings (if any)
    - Any issues or concerns
```

input.description: Read spec reviewer and code quality reviewer templates

content.content:
# Spec Compliance Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a spec compliance reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementer built what was requested (nothing more, nothing less)

```
Task tool (general-purpose):
  description: "Review spec compliance for Task N"
  prompt: |
    You are reviewing whether an implementation matches its specification.

    ## What Was Requested

    [FULL TEXT of task requirements]

    ## What Implementer Claims They Built

    [From implementer's report]

    ## CRITICAL: Do Not Trust the Report

    The implementer finished suspiciously quickly. Their report may be incomplete,
    inaccurate, or optimistic. You MUST verify everything independently.

    **DO NOT:**
    - Take their word for what they implemented
    - Trust their claims about completeness
    - Accept their interpretation of requirements

    **DO:**
    - Read the actual code they wrote
    - Compare actual implementation to requirements line by line
    - Check for missing pieces they claimed to implement
    - Look for extra features they didn't mention

    ## Your Job

    Read the implementation code and verify:

    **Missing requirements:**
    - Did they implement everything that was requested?
    - Are there requirements they skipped or missed?
    - Did they claim something works but didn't actually implement it?

    **Extra/unneeded work:**
    - Did they build things that weren't requested?
    - Did they over-engineer or add unnecessary features?
    - Did they add "nice to haves" that weren't in spec?

    **Misunderstandings:**
    - Did they interpret requirements differently than intended?
    - Did they solve the wrong problem?
    - Did they implement the right feature but wrong way?

    **Verify by reading code, not by trusting report.**

    Report:
    - ✅ Spec compliant (if everything matches after code inspection)
    - ❌ Issues found: [list specifically what's missing or extra, with file:line references]
```
===CODE_QUALITY===
# Code Quality Reviewer Prompt Template

Use this template when dispatching a code quality reviewer subagent.

**Purpose:** Verify implementation is well-built (clean, tested, maintainable)

**Only dispatch after spec compliance review passes.**

```
Task tool (superpowers:code-reviewer):
  Use template at requesting-code-review/code-reviewer.md

  WHAT_WAS_IMPLEMENTED: [from implementer's report]
  PLAN_OR_REQUIREMENTS: Task N from [plan-file]
  BASE_SHA: [commit before task]
  HEAD_SHA: [current commit]
  DESCRIPTION: [task summary]
```

**Code reviewer returns:** Strengths, Issues (Critical/Important/Minor), Assessment

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 1 of Week 6.5a of ax-next: scaffold the `@ax/ipc-protocol` package — a types-only Zod-schemas package that is the single source of truth for the host↔sandbox wire surface.

## Repository

Work from `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next`. The branch `feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift` is already checked out off `main`. Baseline: `pnpm build && pnpm test` all green.

The full plan lives at `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5a-topology-shift.md` and the design contract at `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md`. The design doc's "IPC protocol" and "Error model" sections are the spec you're implementing.

## Invariants for this slice (must respect)

- **I2** — No half-wired packages. `@ax/ipc-protocol` is types-only with no runtime wiring; every schema it exports is consumed by a later-task plugin.
- **I4** — No leaky hook-payload field names. Use generic terms: `cursor` not `rowid`, `version` not `sha`, `token` not `jwt`, no git/sqlite/pg vocabulary.
- **I11** — The `MAX_FRAME` cap (4 MiB) from `@ax/core/src/ipc/framing.ts` stays the body-size ceiling (you don't wire it here, just don't invent a new cap).

## What to build

### Files
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/package.json`
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json`
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts` (re-exports)
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts` (sandbox→host RPC request/response schemas)
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts` (sandbox→host fire-and-forget event schemas)
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts` (error-code enum + error body schema)
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts` (per-action timeout defaults)
- Create `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`
- Modify root `tsconfig.json` to add a `{ "path": "packages/ipc-protocol" }` reference

### Scaffold conventions
Look at `packages/sandbox-subprocess/package.json` and `packages/sandbox-subprocess/tsconfig.json` for the exact shape other plugins use: ESM, `type: "module"`, vitest scripts, `"files": ["dist"]`, `"main": "./dist/index.js"` and matching `"types"` + `"exports"`. Copy that exactly.

**Dependencies:** `zod` only (look at what version `@ax/sandbox-subprocess` pins and match it exactly — use the same string including the `^` if that's what siblings use). Dev dep: `vitest`, `typescript`, `@types/node` as siblings do. NO dependency on `@ax/core` — this package is wire-layer and must not depend on kernel types. Re-declare anything you need (ChatMessage shape, ToolCall shape, ToolDescriptor shape) locally. This is deliberate — the host-side plugins and sandbox-side runner both import `@ax/ipc-protocol`; independence from `@ax/core` lets sandbox-side packages avoid pulling in the kernel.

### Schema surface (from design doc §"Sandbox → host actions (sync RPC)")

**`LlmCallRequestSchema` / `LlmCallResponseSchema`** — mirrors the shape `@ax/core` uses for `LlmRequest`/`LlmResponse` but declared locally:
- Request: `{ messages: ChatMessage[], tools?: ToolDescriptor[], model?: string, maxTokens?: number, temperature?: number }`
- `ChatMessage`: `{ role: 'user' | 'assistant' | 'system', content: string }`
- `ToolCall`: `{ id: string, name: string, input: unknown }`
- `ToolDescriptor`: `{ name: string, description?: string, inputSchema: Record<string, unknown>, executesIn: 'sandbox' | 'host' }`
- Response: `{ assistantMessage: ChatMessage, toolCalls: ToolCall[], stopReason?: string, usage?: { inputTokens?: number, outputTokens?: number } }`

**`ToolPreCallRequestSchema` / `ToolPreCallResponseSchema`** — payload `{ call: ToolCall }`. Response is a discriminated union: `{ verdict: 'allow', modifiedCall?: ToolCall } | { verdict: 'reject', reason: string }`.

**`ToolExecuteHostRequestSchema` / `ToolExecuteHostResponseSchema`** — payload `{ call: ToolCall }` → `{ output: unknown }`. (No runtime wiring for this in 6.5a; schema only.)

**`ToolListRequestSchema` / `ToolListResponseSchema`** — `{}` → `{ tools: ToolDescriptor[] }`.

**`WorkspaceCommitNotifyRequestSchema` / `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`** — Request `{ parentVersion: string | null, commitRef: string, message: string }`. Response discriminated union: `{ accepted: true, version: string, delta: null } | { accepted: false, reason: string }`. Brand the `version` output field as a `WorkspaceVersion` opaque token via TypeScript:

```ts
export type WorkspaceVersion = string & { readonly __brand: 'WorkspaceVersion' };
```

At runtime it's just a string; the brand is documentation for callers to treat it as opaque. JSDoc-comment the type saying "Opaque token. Pass to workspace hooks; never parse."

**`SessionNextMessageResponseSchema`** — response-only (request is a GET with a query-string cursor, which you do NOT Zod-validate in the protocol package; document with JSDoc):
- Discriminated union on `type`:
  - `{ type: 'user-message', payload: ChatMessage, cursor: number }`
  - `{ type: 'cancel', cursor: number }`
  - `{ type: 'timeout', cursor: number }`

### Events (design doc §"Sandbox → host events (fire-and-forget)")

**`EventStreamChunkSchema`** — `{ reqId: string, text: string, kind: 'text' | 'thinking' }`. Schema only; no runtime wiring in 6.5a (it's for 6.5b).

**`EventToolPostCallSchema`** — `{ call: ToolCall, output: unknown, durationMs?: number }`.

**`EventTurnEndSchema`** — `{ reqId?: string, reason: 'user-message-wait' | 'error' | 'complete', usage?: { inputTokens?: number, outputTokens?: number } }`.

**`EventChatEndSchema`** — `{ outcome: ChatOutcome }` where `ChatOutcome` is the discriminated union:
- `{ kind: 'complete', messages: ChatMessage[] }`
- `{ kind: 'terminated', reason: string, error?: unknown }`

(Match the shape `@ax/core/src/types.ts` exports for `ChatOutcome`, but declared locally in this package.)

### Errors (design doc §"Error model")

```ts
export const IpcErrorCodeSchema = z.enum([
  'SESSION_INVALID',
  'HOST_UNAVAILABLE',
  'VALIDATION',
  'HOOK_REJECTED',
  'INTERNAL',
]);
export const IpcErrorSchema = z.object({
  code: IpcErrorCodeSchema,
  message: z.string(),
});
export const IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema = z.object({
  error: IpcErrorSchema,
});
```

### Timeouts

```ts
export const IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS = Object.freeze({
  'llm.call': 5 * 60_000,
  'tool.pre-call': 10_000,
  'tool.execute-host': 30_000,
  'tool.list': 5_000,
  'workspace.commit-notify': 30_000,
  'session.next-message': 30_000,
});
```

Export a `type IpcActionName = keyof typeof IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS`.

## Testing (TDD)

For each schema group, write a test FIRST that fails with "module not found" or similar, then implement:

1. `describe('llm.call')` — accepts minimal request, rejects unknown role, round-trips a response with tool calls.
2. `describe('tool.pre-call')` — accepts allow + modifiedCall, accepts reject + reason, rejects invalid verdict.
3. `describe('tool.execute-host')` — round-trips a request/response.
4. `describe('tool.list')` — round-trips empty + populated catalog; descriptor requires `executesIn`.
5. `describe('workspace.commit-notify')` — accepts accepted shape (version + delta:null), accepts rejected shape (reason), rejects malformed.
6. `describe('session.next-message response')` — round-trips all three type variants, rejects unknown type.
7. `describe('events')` — each event schema round-trips a valid payload and rejects an obviously malformed one.
8. `describe('errors')` — enum covers the five codes (regression guard: test asserts the exact set).
9. `describe('timeouts')` — `IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` is frozen and contains the six expected keys.

Use vitest; file at `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`. Tests import from `'../actions.js'`, `'../events.js'`, etc. (ESM `.js` extensions on relative imports — that's the repo's convention, check `packages/core/src/__tests__/*.test.ts` for examples).

## Commands

- Test: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol test`
- Build: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol build` (or full `pnpm build` at the end)
- Root tsconfig reference: add `{ "path": "packages/ipc-protocol" }` to `tsconfig.json`'s `references` array.

## Invocation flow (TDD, one commit per task)

1. Scaffold `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, root tsconfig reference. `pnpm install` if needed.
2. Write the failing test file with ALL test cases stubbed (using `it.todo` or skipped initially is fine, but each implemented test is a red→green cycle).
3. For each schema group, unskip/add tests, run `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol test` expecting FAIL, then implement in the matching `.ts` file, then confirm PASS.
4. `pnpm build` at the end to confirm the whole monorepo still builds.
5. Commit with message:
```
feat(ipc-protocol): Zod schemas for host↔sandbox wire surface
```
Include `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer.

## Self-review before reporting back

- **Completeness:** every action + event + error + timeout from the design doc has a schema.
- **Quality:** no `z.any()` except where `unknown` input/output is genuinely the contract (`ToolCall.input`, `ToolDescriptor.inputSchema`, `EventToolPostCall.output`). Use `.strict()` on objects where unknown fields should be rejected (e.g., error envelope); allow unknown on envelopes that will evolve (most request/response bodies — document your choice).
- **Discipline:** no runtime logic beyond `Object.freeze` on the timeouts. No `@ax/core` import. No schema beyond what's in the design doc §"IPC protocol."
- **Testing:** each test verifies behavior, not just that Zod exists. Assert actual parsed output, not just that `.parse()` doesn't throw.

## Report back

When done, report:
- Files created (list with one-line purpose each).
- Test counts (file count, total tests, all passing).
- Anything you couldn't answer from the task description (ideally none).

You have full write access. Work in `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next`. Do not touch any file outside the paths listed above. If something unexpected comes up (e.g., the `zod` version used by siblings doesn't exist in the lockfile), stop and ask.

content.text:
Task 1 complete. Commit `c8d014c` on `feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift`.

## Files created

- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/package.json` — ESM, private workspace pkg; zod `^3.23.8` matched to siblings, vitest/typescript/@types/node dev deps matched to siblings; no `@ax/core` dependency.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json` — extends `tsconfig.base.json`, excludes `__tests__`, empty `references` (package is leaf in the graph).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts` — barrel re-export of the four submodules.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts` — `LlmCall`, `ToolPreCall`, `ToolExecuteHost`, `ToolList`, `WorkspaceCommitNotify` request/response schemas, plus `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` discriminated union, plus the shared `ChatMessageSchema` / `ToolCallSchema` / `ToolDescriptorSchema` shapes and the branded `WorkspaceVersion` type.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts` — `EventStreamChunk`, `EventToolPostCall`, `EventTurnEnd`, `EventChatEnd` (with local `ChatOutcomeSchema`).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts` — `IpcErrorCodeSchema` (5 codes), `IpcErrorSchema`, `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` (`.strict()` — envelope shape is frozen, extensions go inside `error`).
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts` — frozen `IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` map with 6 actions and `IpcActionName` type.
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` — 39 tests across 10 `describe` blocks.

## Modified

- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/tsconfig.json` — added `{ "path": "packages/ipc-protocol" }` reference.
- `pnpm-lock.yaml` — regenerated for the new workspace package (zod 3.25.76 resolved from `^3.23.8`, same as siblings).

## Tests

- 1 test file, 39 tests, all passing.
- Full monorepo: 12 packages, 195 tests total (154 baseline + 39 new), all green.
- `pnpm build` at the repo root: clean.

## Self-review notes

- **Completeness:** every action, event, error, and timeout from the design-doc IPC section has a schema; no extras.
- **`.strict()` choices:** applied only to `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` (documented: envelope keyset is frozen, extensions go inside `error`). Request/response bodies and events are permissive to allow additive provider knobs and future event fields — documented inline.
- **`z.unknown()` usage:** only on `ToolCall.input`, `ToolDescriptor.inputSchema` (as `z.record(z.unknown())`), `ToolExecuteHostResponse.output`, `EventToolPostCall.output`, and `ChatOutcome.error` — each is genuinely opaque at the wire layer. No `z.any()`.
- **I2:** types-only, no runtime wiring, every export feeds a later 6.5a task (IPC server dispatcher, agent-runner-core inbox, etc.).
- **I4:** no git/sqlite/pg vocabulary — `parentVersion` / `version` / `commitRef` / `cursor`, not `parentSha` / `sha` / `rowid` / `blobId`. `WorkspaceVersion` is JSDoc-branded as "Opaque token. Pass to workspace hooks; never parse."
- **I11:** no body-size cap introduced; deferred to `@ax/core/src/ipc/framing.ts` `MAX_FRAME`.
- **No `@ax/core` dep:** verified in `package.json`; `ChatMessage` / `ToolCall` / `ToolDescriptor` / `ChatOutcome` redeclared locally with a comment explaining the intentional boundary.

Nothing in the task description went unanswered.

Export a `type IpcActionName = keyof typeof IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS`.

6. `describe('session.next-message response')` — round-trips all three type variants, rejects unknown type.
7. `describe('events')` — each event schema round-trips a valid payload and rejects an obviously malformed one.
8. `describe('errors')` — enum covers the five codes (regression guard: test asserts the exact set).

- Test: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol test`
- Build: `pnpm --filter @ax/ipc-protocol build` (or full `pnpm build` at the end)

1. Scaffold `package.json`, `tsconfig.json`, root tsconfig reference. `pnpm install` if needed.


## Self-review before reporting back

- **Completeness:** every action + event + error + timeout from the design doc has a schema.
- **Quality:** no `z.any()` except where `unknown` input/output is genuinely the contract (`ToolCall.input`, `ToolDescriptor.inputSchema`, `EventToolPostCall.output`). 11. **No `@ax/core` import anywhere** in `src/`. Grep for it.

Things that commonly slip:

You are reviewing the code quality of a single-commit change that scaffolds `@ax/ipc-protocol`, a types-only Zod-schemas package for the host↔sandbox IPC wire surface in ax-next.

## Context

- Repo: `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next`
- Branch: `feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift` (off `main`)
- Commit to review: `c8d014c`
- Baseline commit (diff against): `HEAD~1` (the first commit on this branch — a plan-doc-only commit that would be the prior state; however `docs/plans` is gitignored, so in practice this is the first code commit). Diff against `main` for the code comparison: `git diff main...feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift -- packages/ipc-protocol tsconfig.json pnpm-lock.yaml`.
- Spec compliance review already passed (all 13 items, no mismatches).

## What was implemented

Types-only Zod schemas for the host↔sandbox wire surface:

1. `packages/ipc-protocol/package.json` — ESM, `private: true`, `version: 0.0.0`, dep on `zod ^3.23.8`, devDeps matching siblings.
2. `packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json` — extends `tsconfig.base.json`.
3. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts` — re-exports.
4. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts` — schemas for `llm.call`, `tool.pre-call`, `tool.execute-host`, `tool.list`, `workspace.commit-notify`, `session.next-message`, plus shared shapes (`ChatMessageSchema`, `ToolCallSchema`, `ToolDescriptorSchema`, `WorkspaceVersion` branded type).
5. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts` — schemas for `event.stream-chunk`, `event.tool-post-call`, `event.turn-end`, `event.chat-end`, plus `ChatOutcomeSchema`.
6. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts` — 5-code enum + error body + strict-envelope schemas.
7. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts` — `Object.freeze`d map of 6 action timeouts.
8. `packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts` — 39 tests.
9. Root `tsconfig.json` — added `{"path": "packages/ipc-protocol"}`.
10. `pnpm-lock.yaml` — regenerated.

Deliberately excluded: `@ax/core` dependency. This package must not depend on the kernel because sandbox-side code will also import it; shared shapes (`ChatMessage`, `ToolCall`, `ToolDescriptor`, `ChatOutcome`) are redeclared locally rather than imported.

## Standards this repo follows (partial — check CLAUDE.md + ax-conventions skill for the full list)

- **The five invariants** in `CLAUDE.md`: transport/storage-agnostic hook surface; no cross-plugin imports; no half-wired plugins; one source of truth per concept; capabilities explicit and minimized.
- **`eslint.config.mjs`** enforces `no-restricted-imports` for `@ax/*` except `@ax/core` + `@ax/test-harness`. This slice will grow that allowlist (for `@ax/ipc-protocol` + `@ax/agent-runner-core`) in Task 8, but for NOW the package must not be imported from any plugin code. It's OK for this package to be sitting unused by consumers — consumers land in Tasks 2–13.
- **`packages/sandbox-subprocess/package.json`** is the reference for package scaffolding shape.
- **`packages/sandbox-subprocess/SECURITY.md`** is the reference for security notes on trust-boundary packages (not needed for this package — no runtime, no FS/net/process surface).
- ESM with explicit `.js` extensions on relative imports.
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` is on in `tsconfig.base.json` — schemas that emit `| undefined` for optional fields need care.
- Test file naming: `*.test.ts` under `__tests__/` subdirs.

## What to review

Please produce a full code review focused on:

1. **Package manifest / tsconfig hygiene.** Does `package.json` follow the sibling shape exactly? Is the `zod` version pinned to what other packages use? Are devDeps in sync? Does `tsconfig.json` set the right `outDir` / `rootDir` / `composite` flags for a monorepo reference?

2. **Zod usage correctness.** Are discriminated unions using `z.discriminatedUnion` (not `z.union`)? Are required vs optional fields correct relative to the design doc? Are schemas using `.strict()` or default-permissive appropriately — and is the choice documented? Is the `WorkspaceVersion` brand done at the TypeScript-type level (not a runtime Zod `.brand()` — which would produce a different parse output type)?

3. **Type exports and ergonomics.** Are type aliases (`z.infer` / `z.input`) exported alongside the schemas? Are the branded types usable (i.e., callers can construct one via an explicit cast, documented in a JSDoc)? Is there any naming collision between a schema and its inferred type?

4. **Boundary cleanliness.** No `@ax/core` import in `src/`. No git/sqlite/pg/k8s vocabulary in payload field names (`parentVersion` not `parentSha`; `version` not `sha`; `cursor` not `rowid`). This is CLAUDE.md invariant I1.

5. **Test quality.** Are tests asserting actual behavior (parsed output shapes), not just `.parse()` not throwing? Are discriminated-union tests covering all arms? Do tests assert the frozen timeouts object is actually frozen (attempting a mutation should throw in strict mode)? Does the error-enum test guard against enum drift?

6. **Dead code / over-building.** Any schema or export that isn't required by the spec and isn't consumed by a later task? Any `z.any()` that should be `z.unknown()`? Any unnecessary runtime logic (this is meant to be a pure-schemas package)?

7. **JSDoc and inline comments.** Is the `WorkspaceVersion` type JSDoc-commented as "opaque; never parse"? Is the deliberate `@ax/core`-independence documented somewhere? Do permissive-vs-strict choices have a reason comment?

## Files to focus on

```
packages/ipc-protocol/package.json
packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json
packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts
tsconfig.json
```

Ignore `pnpm-lock.yaml` — regenerated by pnpm, not reviewable.

## Out of scope

- Spec compliance (already passed separately — `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` having a `timeout` variant, etc.).
- Whether `@ax/agent-runner-core` can import this (wired in a later task).
- Whether the runtime IPC server exists (Tasks 3–4).
- Security review of a network listener (no listener here).

## Output

Report:
- **Strengths** (what was done well)
- **Issues — Critical** (must fix before merge)
- **Issues — Important** (should fix before merge)
- **Issues — Minor** (nice to fix)
- **Assessment** — approved / needs-rework

Use file:line references for every issue.

content.text:
Confirmed: `"strip"` — strips unknown keys silently. If this is meant to assert empty request, it should be `.strict()`. Minor nit.

I have enough. Let me finalize the review.

---

# Code Review: `@ax/ipc-protocol` scaffold (commit `c8d014c`)

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/package.json`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/tsconfig.json`

---

## Strengths

- **Boundary cleanliness (I1, I5) is excellent.** The boundary-review comment in `actions.ts:141-148` explicitly calls out `parentVersion`/`version`/`commitRef`/`delta:null` as the non-git vocabulary, with I1 cited. No sqlite/pg/k8s/git vocabulary in payload field names. The "deliberately redeclared here rather than imported from `@ax/core`" comment at `actions.ts:5-11` captures the sandbox-side independence rationale per the task brief.
- **`.strict()` vs permissive is a deliberate, documented choice.** `actions.ts:60-65` and `events.ts:11-13` explain why envelopes are permissive (forward-compatible optional knobs) while `errors.ts:26-31` explains why `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` is strict (frozen shape; drift is a protocol bug). This is exactly the reasoning the brief asks for.
- **Discriminated unions used correctly everywhere** — `z.discriminatedUnion` for `ToolPreCallResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:98`), `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:159`), `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:188`), `ChatOutcomeSchema` (`events.ts:59`). No `z.union` where `discriminatedUnion` would be correct.
- **`WorkspaceVersion` brand is a TypeScript-type brand, not a runtime Zod `.brand()`** (`actions.ts:55`). Matches the brief's explicit ask — runtime parse output stays `string`, the brand is documentation. JSDoc says "never parse."
- **Error-enum drift guard test is in place** (`schemas.test.ts:341-352`) — any addition/removal of a code requires updating the expected array. Per-code documentation in `errors.ts:3-9` also names the test as the guard.
- **Timeouts are frozen at module load and asserted frozen** (`timeouts.ts:11`, `schemas.test.ts:379-381`), with keyset coverage also asserted (`schemas.test.ts:383-393`). Good regression surface.
- **Type aliases co-exported with every schema** via `z.infer`. No naming collisions — schemas suffixed `Schema`, inferred types without.
- **39 tests exercise shape semantics**, not just non-throw: parsed fields are asserted, all discriminated-union arms have both accept and reject cases, `executesIn` requirement is exercised, nullable fields are exercised (`parentVersion: null`).
- **No `z.any()`** — every opaque field is `z.unknown()` (`actions.ts:27`, `:124`; `events.ts:34`, `:67`). Correct.

---

## Issues — Critical (90-100)

None.

---

## Issues — Important (80-89)

### 1. `WorkspaceVersion` brand is dead code at this slice (confidence 82)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:55`

The `WorkspaceVersion` type is declared and exported but never applied anywhere in the schemas. `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema.version` (line 164) and `WorkspaceCommitNotifyRequestSchema.parentVersion` (line 151) both use `z.string()` / `z.string().nullable()`, producing bare `string` in the inferred types. The test at `schemas.test.ts:173-183` also treats `version` as a plain `string`. Nothing else in this commit or the repo consumes the brand.

This trips the "any export not required by the spec and not consumed by a later task" filter in the review brief, and also leaves a gap where a careless consumer in Task 2/3 will mis-type the wire field as raw `string` before anything guides them to the brand.

**Fix (pick one):**
- Remove `WorkspaceVersion` from this slice and reintroduce it in the task where the host's workspace plugin actually hands back a branded value (e.g., Task 5 or wherever workspace hooks land).
- Or apply it now: export a typed helper (`version` field typed as `WorkspaceVersion` via a `.transform(v => v as WorkspaceVersion)` on the schema, or a separate `asWorkspaceVersion(s: string): WorkspaceVersion` helper) and use it in `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`. If you keep it, the JSDoc should also document the intended construction path (cast at the host boundary, never by sandbox code).

Currently it's in the middle ground: exported as public API but with nothing to enforce or even demonstrate use.

---

## Issues — Minor (≥80, nice to fix)

### 2. `ToolListRequestSchema` is `strip`, not `strict` (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:132`

`z.object({})` defaults to `"strip"` mode — unknown keys are silently dropped, not rejected. Because `tool.list` has no request parameters at all, a strict empty schema would be the more defensive choice: any consumer stuffing a field into the request body is misusing the protocol and should hear about it.

The test at `schemas.test.ts:136` (`ToolListRequestSchema.parse({})`) doesn't exercise the unknown-key case, so the strip behavior is invisible. The `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` in `errors.ts:32-36` sets the precedent for `.strict()` on fixed envelopes.

**Fix:** `export const ToolListRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();` and add a test that a request with an extra field rejects.

---

### 3. Missing `vitest.config.ts` (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/` (no file)

Every other package in the monorepo has a `vitest.config.ts` pinning `test.include` to `src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts` (verified across `sandbox-subprocess`, `tool-bash`, `tool-dispatcher`, `core`, `llm-mock`, `audit-log`, `cli`, `llm-anthropic`, `storage-sqlite`, `test-harness`, `tool-file-io`). Tests currently pass because vitest's default discovery catches `*.test.ts`, but this is silent drift from the sibling shape and means the explicit include guard is absent — e.g., if anyone ever adds a `*.test.ts` under `dist/` or outside `src/__tests__/`, behavior will diverge from siblings.

**Fix:** Add a minimal vitest.config.ts matching the majority sibling shape:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';
export default defineConfig({ test: { include: ['src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts'] } });
```

---

### 4. Timeouts map lacks an explicit satisfies/typing guard (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts:11-18`

`IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` is an untyped `Object.freeze(...)` call. The inferred value type is `number`, and `keyof typeof IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` gives the literal keys, so it works — but only because `Object.freeze` preserves the inferred literal shape. An accidental widening (e.g., someone editing to `as number`) would silently break `IpcActionName`.

The existing test at `schemas.test.ts:395-406` is a runtime type-echo, not a compile-time check; it doesn't actually catch type widening because all six strings are re-asserted as `IpcActionName[]`.

**Fix (optional, small):** Add `satisfies Readonly<Record<string, number>>` on the object so editors surface drift, without changing the inferred keyof.

```ts
export const IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS = Object.freeze({
  'llm.call': 5 * 60_000,
  // ...
} satisfies Record<string, number>);
```

---

### 5. `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema.cursor` semantics aren't captured (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:188-205`

`cursor: z.number().int().nonnegative()` is on all three variants. The comment block at `actions.ts:177-186` documents the request shape (HTTP GET with `?cursor=<n>`) but not the response-side cursor semantics — is it the same cursor the client sent (echo for correlation)? The next cursor to send (advance-by-one)? The server's current high-water mark? Subscribers will read this differently depending on what the inbox loop in Task 8 decides.

This is the kind of hook-surface field name that future consumers will key off, and the boundary-review checklist specifically flags that risk.

**Fix:** Add a one-line JSDoc on each arm's `cursor` (or on the schema block) stating "server echoes the cursor the client should use on the NEXT request" (or whichever the semantics is). Cheap to write now, expensive to reconcile once Task 2 and Task 8 both have opinions.

---

## Assessment

**Approved with minor follow-ups.**

The package is clean, well-commented, and correctly factored. The five invariants are respected: I1 (storage-agnostic — vocabulary is abstract), I2 (no `@ax/core` import), I3 (not half-wired — this is a types-only package and isn't expected to be consumed until Task 8, which the brief explicitly carves out), I4 (single source of truth — shared shapes are declared once here, with a comment acknowledging drift-vs-`@ax/core`), I5 (capabilities: no runtime, no FS/net/process surface, so no capability grants to minimize).

None of the issues above are merge-blocking. Item 1 (`WorkspaceVersion` dead export) is the one I'd most want addressed before landing — either apply the brand in this slice or defer it to the task that earns it — but it's just past the "Minor" line, not a critical bug. Items 2-5 are polish.

The follow-up, per the memory note about targeted commits, is probably best done as a single cleanup commit on this branch rather than amending `c8d014c`.

- **The five invariants** in `CLAUDE.md`: transport/storage-agnostic hook surface; no cross-plugin imports; no half-wired plugins; one source of truth per concept; capabilities explicit and minimized.
- **`eslint.config.mjs`** enforces `no-restricted-imports` for `@ax/*` except `@ax/core` + `@ax/test-harness`. This slice will grow that allowlist (for `@ax/ipc-protocol` + `@ax/agent-runner-core`) in Task 8, but for NOW the package must not be imported from any plugin code. It's OK for this package to be sitting unused by consumers — consumers land in Tasks 2–13.
- **`packages/sandbox-subprocess/package.json`** is the reference for package scaffolding shape.
- **`packages/sandbox-subprocess/SECURITY.md`** is the reference for security notes on trust-boundary packages (not needed for this package — no runtime, no FS/net/process surface).
- ESM with explicit `.js` extensions on relative imports.
- `exactOptionalPropertyTypes: true` is on in `tsconfig.base.json` — schemas that emit `| undefined` for optional fields need care.
- Test file naming: `*.test.ts` under `__tests__/` subdirs.

## What to review

Please produce a full code review focused on:

1. **Package manifest / tsconfig hygiene.** Does `package.json` follow the sibling shape exactly? Is the `zod` version pinned to what other packages use? Are devDeps in sync? Does `tsconfig.json` set the right `outDir` / `rootDir` / `composite` flags for a monorepo reference?

2. **Zod usage correctness.** Are discriminated unions using `z.discriminatedUnion` (not `z.union`)? Are required vs optional fields correct relative to the design doc? Are schemas using `.strict()` or default-permissive appropriately — and is the choice documented? Is the `WorkspaceVersion` brand done at the TypeScript-type level (not a runtime Zod `.brand()` — which would produce a different parse output type)?

3. **Type exports and ergonomics.** Are type aliases (`z.infer` / `z.input`) exported alongside the schemas? Are the branded types usable (i.e., callers can construct one via an explicit cast, documented in a JSDoc)? Is there any naming collision between a schema and its inferred type?

4. **Boundary cleanliness.** No `@ax/core` import in `src/`. No git/sqlite/pg/k8s vocabulary in payload field names (`parentVersion` not `parentSha`; `version` not `sha`; `cursor` not `rowid`). This is CLAUDE.md invariant I1.

5. **Test quality.** Are tests asserting actual behavior (parsed output shapes), not just `.parse()` not throwing? Are discriminated-union tests covering all arms? Do tests assert the frozen timeouts object is actually frozen (attempting a mutation should throw in strict mode)? Does the error-enum test guard against enum drift?

6. **Dead code / over-building.** Any schema or export that isn't required by the spec and isn't consumed by a later task? Any `z.any()` that should be `z.unknown()`? Any unnecessary runtime logic (this is meant to be a pure-schemas package)?

7. **JSDoc and inline comments.** Is the `WorkspaceVersion` type JSDoc-commented as "opaque; never parse"? Is the deliberate `@ax/core`-independence documented somewhere? Do permissive-vs-strict choices have a reason comment?

## Files to focus on

```
packages/ipc-protocol/package.json
packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json
packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts
packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts
tsconfig.json
```

Ignore `pnpm-lock.yaml` — regenerated by pnpm, not reviewable.

## Out of scope

- Spec compliance (already passed separately — `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` having a `timeout` variant, etc.).
- Whether `@ax/agent-runner-core` can import this (wired in a later task).
- Whether the runtime IPC server exists (Tasks 3–4).
- Security review of a network listener (no listener here).

## Output

Report:
- **Strengths** (what was done well)
- **Issues — Critical** (must fix before merge)
- **Issues — Important** (should fix before merge)
- **Issues — Minor** (nice to fix)
- **Assessment** — approved / needs-rework

Use file:line references for every issue.

content.text:
Confirmed: `"strip"` — strips unknown keys silently. If this is meant to assert empty request, it should be `.strict()`. Minor nit.

I have enough. Let me finalize the review.

---

# Code Review: `@ax/ipc-protocol` scaffold (commit `c8d014c`)

**Files reviewed:**
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/package.json`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/tsconfig.json`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/index.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/events.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/errors.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/__tests__/schemas.test.ts`
- `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/tsconfig.json`

---

## Strengths

- **Boundary cleanliness (I1, I5) is excellent.** The boundary-review comment in `actions.ts:141-148` explicitly calls out `parentVersion`/`version`/`commitRef`/`delta:null` as the non-git vocabulary, with I1 cited. No sqlite/pg/k8s/git vocabulary in payload field names. The "deliberately redeclared here rather than imported from `@ax/core`" comment at `actions.ts:5-11` captures the sandbox-side independence rationale per the task brief.
- **`.strict()` vs permissive is a deliberate, documented choice.** `actions.ts:60-65` and `events.ts:11-13` explain why envelopes are permissive (forward-compatible optional knobs) while `errors.ts:26-31` explains why `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` is strict (frozen shape; drift is a protocol bug). This is exactly the reasoning the brief asks for.
- **Discriminated unions used correctly everywhere** — `z.discriminatedUnion` for `ToolPreCallResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:98`), `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:159`), `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` (`actions.ts:188`), `ChatOutcomeSchema` (`events.ts:59`). No `z.union` where `discriminatedUnion` would be correct.
- **`WorkspaceVersion` brand is a TypeScript-type brand, not a runtime Zod `.brand()`** (`actions.ts:55`). Matches the brief's explicit ask — runtime parse output stays `string`, the brand is documentation. JSDoc says "never parse."
- **Error-enum drift guard test is in place** (`schemas.test.ts:341-352`) — any addition/removal of a code requires updating the expected array. Per-code documentation in `errors.ts:3-9` also names the test as the guard.
- **Timeouts are frozen at module load and asserted frozen** (`timeouts.ts:11`, `schemas.test.ts:379-381`), with keyset coverage also asserted (`schemas.test.ts:383-393`). Good regression surface.
- **Type aliases co-exported with every schema** via `z.infer`. No naming collisions — schemas suffixed `Schema`, inferred types without.
- **39 tests exercise shape semantics**, not just non-throw: parsed fields are asserted, all discriminated-union arms have both accept and reject cases, `executesIn` requirement is exercised, nullable fields are exercised (`parentVersion: null`).
- **No `z.any()`** — every opaque field is `z.unknown()` (`actions.ts:27`, `:124`; `events.ts:34`, `:67`). Correct.

---

## Issues — Critical (90-100)

None.

---

## Issues — Important (80-89)

### 1. `WorkspaceVersion` brand is dead code at this slice (confidence 82)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:55`

The `WorkspaceVersion` type is declared and exported but never applied anywhere in the schemas. `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema.version` (line 164) and `WorkspaceCommitNotifyRequestSchema.parentVersion` (line 151) both use `z.string()` / `z.string().nullable()`, producing bare `string` in the inferred types. The test at `schemas.test.ts:173-183` also treats `version` as a plain `string`. Nothing else in this commit or the repo consumes the brand.

This trips the "any export not required by the spec and not consumed by a later task" filter in the review brief, and also leaves a gap where a careless consumer in Task 2/3 will mis-type the wire field as raw `string` before anything guides them to the brand.

**Fix (pick one):**
- Remove `WorkspaceVersion` from this slice and reintroduce it in the task where the host's workspace plugin actually hands back a branded value (e.g., Task 5 or wherever workspace hooks land).
- Or apply it now: export a typed helper (`version` field typed as `WorkspaceVersion` via a `.transform(v => v as WorkspaceVersion)` on the schema, or a separate `asWorkspaceVersion(s: string): WorkspaceVersion` helper) and use it in `WorkspaceCommitNotifyResponseSchema`. If you keep it, the JSDoc should also document the intended construction path (cast at the host boundary, never by sandbox code).

Currently it's in the middle ground: exported as public API but with nothing to enforce or even demonstrate use.

---

## Issues — Minor (≥80, nice to fix)

### 2. `ToolListRequestSchema` is `strip`, not `strict` (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:132`

`z.object({})` defaults to `"strip"` mode — unknown keys are silently dropped, not rejected. Because `tool.list` has no request parameters at all, a strict empty schema would be the more defensive choice: any consumer stuffing a field into the request body is misusing the protocol and should hear about it.

The test at `schemas.test.ts:136` (`ToolListRequestSchema.parse({})`) doesn't exercise the unknown-key case, so the strip behavior is invisible. The `IpcErrorEnvelopeSchema` in `errors.ts:32-36` sets the precedent for `.strict()` on fixed envelopes.

**Fix:** `export const ToolListRequestSchema = z.object({}).strict();` and add a test that a request with an extra field rejects.

---

### 3. Missing `vitest.config.ts` (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/` (no file)

Every other package in the monorepo has a `vitest.config.ts` pinning `test.include` to `src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts` (verified across `sandbox-subprocess`, `tool-bash`, `tool-dispatcher`, `core`, `llm-mock`, `audit-log`, `cli`, `llm-anthropic`, `storage-sqlite`, `test-harness`, `tool-file-io`). Tests currently pass because vitest's default discovery catches `*.test.ts`, but this is silent drift from the sibling shape and means the explicit include guard is absent — e.g., if anyone ever adds a `*.test.ts` under `dist/` or outside `src/__tests__/`, behavior will diverge from siblings.

**Fix:** Add a minimal vitest.config.ts matching the majority sibling shape:

```ts
import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config';
export default defineConfig({ test: { include: ['src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts'] } });
```

---

### 4. Timeouts map lacks an explicit satisfies/typing guard (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/timeouts.ts:11-18`

`IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` is an untyped `Object.freeze(...)` call. The inferred value type is `number`, and `keyof typeof IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS` gives the literal keys, so it works — but only because `Object.freeze` preserves the inferred literal shape. An accidental widening (e.g., someone editing to `as number`) would silently break `IpcActionName`.

The existing test at `schemas.test.ts:395-406` is a runtime type-echo, not a compile-time check; it doesn't actually catch type widening because all six strings are re-asserted as `IpcActionName[]`.

**Fix (optional, small):** Add `satisfies Readonly<Record<string, number>>` on the object so editors surface drift, without changing the inferred keyof.

```ts
export const IPC_TIMEOUTS_MS = Object.freeze({
  'llm.call': 5 * 60_000,
  // ...
} satisfies Record<string, number>);
```

---

### 5. `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema.cursor` semantics aren't captured (confidence 80)

**File:** `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next/packages/ipc-protocol/src/actions.ts:188-205`

`cursor: z.number().int().nonnegative()` is on all three variants. The comment block at `actions.ts:177-186` documents the request shape (HTTP GET with `?cursor=<n>`) but not the response-side cursor semantics — is it the same cursor the client sent (echo for correlation)? The next cursor to send (advance-by-one)? The server's current high-water mark? Subscribers will read this differently depending on what the inbox loop in Task 8 decides.

This is the kind of hook-surface field name that future consumers will key off, and the boundary-review checklist specifically flags that risk.

**Fix:** Add a one-line JSDoc on each arm's `cursor` (or on the schema block) stating "server echoes the cursor the client should use on the NEXT request" (or whichever the semantics is). Cheap to write now, expensive to reconcile once Task 2 and Task 8 both have opinions.

---

## Assessment

**Approved with minor follow-ups.**

The package is clean, well-commented, and correctly factored. The five invariants are respected: I1 (storage-agnostic — vocabulary is abstract), I2 (no `@ax/core` import), I3 (not half-wired — this is a types-only package and isn't expected to be consumed until Task 8, which the brief explicitly carves out), I4 (single source of truth — shared shapes are declared once here, with a comment acknowledging drift-vs-`@ax/core`), I5 (capabilities: no runtime, no FS/net/process surface, so no capability grants to minimize).

None of the issues above are merge-blocking. Item 1 (`WorkspaceVersion` dead export) is the one I'd most want addressed before landing — either apply the brand in this slice or defer it to the task that earns it — but it's just past the "Minor" line, not a critical bug. Items 2-5 are polish.

The follow-up, per the memory note about targeted commits, is probably best done as a single cleanup commit on this branch rather than amending `c8d014c`.
Review returned "Approved" with 1 Important (dead `WorkspaceVersion` brand) + 4 Minor items. Applying targeted follow-up commit for the concrete fixes (items 1, 2, 3, 5).

input.new_string:
/**
 * Opaque handle for a workspace commit as observed by the host.
 * At runtime this is just a string; the brand is documentation.
 *
 * Opaque token. Pass to workspace hooks; never parse.
 *
 * Construct via {@link asWorkspaceVersion} at the host-side boundary
 * (where a backend plugin mints the concrete value). The wire schema
 * applies the brand automatically via transform so parsed responses
 * carry it without callers casting.
 */
export type WorkspaceVersion = string & { readonly __brand: 'WorkspaceVersion' };

/**
 * Host-side helper: narrow a raw string from a workspace backend into a
 * `WorkspaceVersion`. Only callers that mint version tokens (workspace
 * plugins) should use this — everyone else receives branded values
 * through the wire schemas.
 */
export const asWorkspaceVersion = (v: string): WorkspaceVersion =>
  v as WorkspaceVersion;

content.subject: Task 0: Branch setup + baseline verify
content.description: Create feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift off main, verify baseline tests green, commit plan doc.

content.subject: Task 1: @ax/ipc-protocol scaffold + schemas
content.description: Create @ax/ipc-protocol package with Zod schemas for all IPC actions, events, errors, and timeouts per design doc. Types-only package, no runtime. TDD.

content.subject: Task 2: @ax/session-inmemory — session store + inbox
content.description: Implement session:create/resolve-token/queue-work/claim-work/terminate with in-memory store and long-poll inbox. TDD.

content.subject: Task 3: @ax/ipc-server scaffold + SECURITY.md + listener
content.description: Create @ax/ipc-server package with SECURITY.md security-checklist walk, unix-socket HTTP listener, auth middleware, body-size cap at 4 MiB. TDD.

content.subject: Task 4: @ax/ipc-server dispatcher + event ingest
content.description: Wire per-action handlers (llm.call, tool.pre-call, tool.list, workspace.commit-notify stub, session.next-message) and event ingest (tool-post-call, turn-end, chat-end). TDD.

content.subject: Task 5: sandbox:open-session extension + SECURITY.md
content.description: Extend @ax/sandbox-subprocess with sandbox:open-session: private tempdir socket, token via session:create, spawn runner binary with env injection, SECURITY.md extension.

content.subject: Task 6: Delete sandbox:spawn
content.description: Remove sandbox:spawn from @ax/sandbox-subprocess and SandboxSpawn* types from @ax/core. Must run AFTER Task 7 to keep verifyCalls green.

content.subject: Task 7: Tool registry refactor (dispatcher + bash + file-io)
content.description: Extend tool-dispatcher with tool:register + tool:list + catalog seal. Refocus tool-bash and tool-file-io to descriptor-only calling tool:register with executesIn:'sandbox'.

content.subject: Task 8: @ax/agent-runner-core — IPC client + inbox loop + local dispatcher
content.description: Sandbox-side library: HTTP client over unix socket with keepalive + retry, inbox long-poll loop, local tool dispatcher. Update eslint allowlist to add @ax/ipc-protocol and @ax/agent-runner-core.

content.subject: Task 9: @ax/tool-bash-impl — sandbox-side bash
content.description: Port hardened bash execution (spawn with shell:false, argv allowlist, env allowlist, timeout, output caps) from the deleted sandbox-subprocess spawn.ts. Register with runner-core's local dispatcher.

content.subject: Task 10: @ax/tool-file-io-impl — sandbox-side read/write
content.description: Port safePath + read_file + write_file from host-side @ax/tool-file-io into a sandbox-side package. Register with runner-core's local dispatcher.

content.subject: Task 11: @ax/agent-native-runner — entry binary + turn loop
content.description: Sandbox-side entry binary (chmod +x, ESM main guard). Turn loop: inbox → llm.call → tool.pre-call → local-or-host tool exec → event.tool-post-call → event.turn-end → event.chat-end. TDD.

content.subject: Task 12: @ax/chat-orchestrator + delete core/chat-loop.ts
content.description: Host-side chat:run: chat:start → session:create → sandbox:open-session → queue user msg → await chat:end event → return outcome. Delete core's chat-loop.ts and registerChatLoop export. Update test-harness withChatLoop default.

content.subject: Task 13: CLI wiring
content.description: Wire session-inmemory + ipc-server + chat-orchestrator into @ax/cli/main.ts. Resolve runner binary via package resolve. Drop registerChatLoop.

content.subject: Task 14: ESLint + tsconfig refs cleanup
content.description: Verify eslint allowlist, tsconfig references, pnpm-workspace. Run full pnpm build/test/lint to confirm everything builds except acceptance test.

content.subject: Task 15: Acceptance test — subprocess topology
content.description: Preserve Week 4-6 library-mode e2e assertions with mocked Anthropic + real bash. Add new assertion: bash's PPID is NOT the host PID (proves loop runs in subprocess).

content.subject: Task 16: Boundary review + changeset + PR notes
content.description: Write boundary-review block for session:* / sandbox:open-session / tool:register-list / IPC protocol. Add changeset. Draft PR description with security-review blocks lifted from SECURITY.md files.

content.subject: Task 17: Final review + open PR
content.description: Full build/test/lint green. Semgrep + gitleaks clean. No chat-loop/sandbox:spawn/registerChatLoop leftovers. Push branch and open PR. Dispatch final code-reviewer.

content.subject: Task 0: Branch setup + baseline verify
content.description: Create feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift off main, verify baseline tests green, commit plan doc.

content.subject: Task 1: @ax/ipc-protocol scaffold + schemas
content.description: Create @ax/ipc-protocol package with Zod schemas for all IPC actions, events, errors, and timeouts per design doc. Types-only package, no runtime. TDD.

content.subject: Task 2: @ax/session-inmemory — session store + inbox
content.description: Implement session:create/resolve-token/queue-work/claim-work/terminate with in-memory store and long-poll inbox. TDD.

content.subject: Task 3: @ax/ipc-server scaffold + SECURITY.md + listener
content.description: Create @ax/ipc-server package with SECURITY.md security-checklist walk, unix-socket HTTP listener, auth middleware, body-size cap at 4 MiB. TDD.

content.subject: Task 4: @ax/ipc-server dispatcher + event ingest
content.description: Wire per-action handlers (llm.call, tool.pre-call, tool.list, workspace.commit-notify stub, session.next-message) and event ingest (tool-post-call, turn-end, chat-end). TDD.

content.subject: Task 5: sandbox:open-session extension + SECURITY.md
content.description: Extend @ax/sandbox-subprocess with sandbox:open-session: private tempdir socket, token via session:create, spawn runner binary with env injection, SECURITY.md extension.

content.subject: Task 6: Delete sandbox:spawn
content.description: Remove sandbox:spawn from @ax/sandbox-subprocess and SandboxSpawn* types from @ax/core. Must run AFTER Task 7 to keep verifyCalls green.

content.subject: Task 7: Tool registry refactor (dispatcher + bash + file-io)
content.description: Extend tool-dispatcher with tool:register + tool:list + catalog seal. Refocus tool-bash and tool-file-io to descriptor-only calling tool:register with executesIn:'sandbox'.

content.subject: Task 8: @ax/agent-runner-core — IPC client + inbox loop + local dispatcher
content.description: Sandbox-side library: HTTP client over unix socket with keepalive + retry, inbox long-poll loop, local tool dispatcher. Update eslint allowlist to add @ax/ipc-protocol and @ax/agent-runner-core.

content.subject: Task 9: @ax/tool-bash-impl — sandbox-side bash
content.description: Port hardened bash execution (spawn with shell:false, argv allowlist, env allowlist, timeout, output caps) from the deleted sandbox-subprocess spawn.ts. Register with runner-core's local dispatcher.

content.subject: Task 10: @ax/tool-file-io-impl — sandbox-side read/write
content.description: Port safePath + read_file + write_file from host-side @ax/tool-file-io into a sandbox-side package. Register with runner-core's local dispatcher.

content.subject: Task 11: @ax/agent-native-runner — entry binary + turn loop
content.description: Sandbox-side entry binary (chmod +x, ESM main guard). Turn loop: inbox → llm.call → tool.pre-call → local-or-host tool exec → event.tool-post-call → event.turn-end → event.chat-end. TDD.

content.subject: Task 12: @ax/chat-orchestrator + delete core/chat-loop.ts
content.description: Host-side chat:run: chat:start → session:create → sandbox:open-session → queue user msg → await chat:end event → return outcome. Delete core's chat-loop.ts and registerChatLoop export. Update test-harness withChatLoop default.

content.subject: Task 13: CLI wiring
content.description: Wire session-inmemory + ipc-server + chat-orchestrator into @ax/cli/main.ts. Resolve runner binary via package resolve. Drop registerChatLoop.

content.subject: Task 14: ESLint + tsconfig refs cleanup
content.description: Verify eslint allowlist, tsconfig references, pnpm-workspace. Run full pnpm build/test/lint to confirm everything builds except acceptance test.

content.subject: Task 15: Acceptance test — subprocess topology
content.description: Preserve Week 4-6 library-mode e2e assertions with mocked Anthropic + real bash. Add new assertion: bash's PPID is NOT the host PID (proves loop runs in subprocess).

content.subject: Task 16: Boundary review + changeset + PR notes
content.description: Write boundary-review block for session:* / sandbox:open-session / tool:register-list / IPC protocol. Add changeset. Draft PR description with security-review blocks lifted from SECURITY.md files.

input.prompt:
You are implementing Task 2 of Week 6.5a of ax-next: scaffold the `@ax/session-inmemory` package — an in-memory session store + per-session long-poll inbox that registers five service hooks on the hook bus.

## Repository

Work from `/Users/vpulim/dev/ai/ax-next`. Branch `feat/week-6.5a-topology-shift` is checked out; Task 1 (`@ax/ipc-protocol`) just landed (commits `c8d014c` + `edea5c3`). Verify before starting: `pnpm build && pnpm test` is green (195+ tests across 12 packages).

Full plan: `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5a-topology-shift.md`. Design contract: `docs/plans/2026-04-24-week-6.5-agent-sandbox-design.md`. The design doc's §"Sandbox → host actions" (cursor semantics) and §"IPC protocol" sections spec this contract.

## Invariants for this slice (must respect)

- **I1** — Observable chat behavior preserved (your package is not on the chat path yet; this is a foundation layer).
- **I4** — No leaky payload field names. Use `cursor` (not `rowid`), `token` (not `jwt`), `sessionId` generic. Will be re-reviewed in Task 16's boundary-review block.
- **I9** — Auth tokens are 32 bytes of `crypto.randomBytes`, base64url-encoded (no padding). Never JWT.
- No postgres / sqlite vocabulary in any hook payload — this package is the in-memory impl; an `@ax/session-postgres` in Week 7–9 must implement the exact same contract.

## What to build

### Files
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/package.json`
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/tsconfig.json`
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/vitest.config.ts`
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/index.ts` (re-exports the plugin factory)
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/plugin.ts` (wires the store+inbox to service hooks)
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/store.ts` (session + token state)
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/inbox.ts` (per-session queue + long-poll wake)
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/types.ts` (`InboxEntry` + hook I/O types)
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/__tests__/store.test.ts`
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/__tests__/inbox.test.ts`
- Create `packages/session-inmemory/src/__tests__/plugin.test.ts`
- Modify root `tsconfig.json` to add `{ "path": "packages/session-inmemory" }` reference

### Scaffold conventions
Copy the shape of `packages/sandbox-subprocess/package.json` and its `tsconfig.json`. ESM, `type: "module"`, `"private": true`, `"version": "0.0.0"`. Script names: `build`, `test`, `test:watch`. Vitest config matches the sibling pattern (`include: ['src/**/__tests__/**/*.test.ts']`).

**Dependencies:** `@ax/core` (for `Plugin`, `HookBus`, `ChatContext`, `PluginError`, `ChatMessage`). Dev deps: vitest, typescript, @types/node as siblings do. No `zod` needed (types are internal; no wire validation here — the IPC server does wire validation separately). NO dependency on `@ax/ipc-protocol` — this package speaks to the IPC server through the bus, not directly over the wire.

**Note on `ChatMessage`:** if importing from `@ax/core` creates a circular concern with later slices, declare a local `ChatMessageLike` interface matching the shape — but prefer the `@ax/core` import if it works cleanly.

### Hooks to register (all service hooks — exactly one producer)

Manifest:

```ts
{
  name: '@ax/session-inmemory',
  version: '0.0.0',
  registers: [
    'session:create',
    'session:resolve-token',
    'session:queue-work',
    'session:claim-work',
    'session:terminate',
  ],
  calls: [],
  subscribes: [],
}
```

### Service hook contracts

- **`session:create`**
  - Input: `{ sessionId: string, workspaceRoot: string }` — caller (orchestrator) provides the sessionId from its own `ChatContext`. `workspaceRoot` is recorded for later resolution.
  - Output: `{ sessionId: string, token: string }` where `token` is 43 base64url chars (32 bytes → 43 chars no padding).
  - Errors: `PluginError(code: 'duplicate-session')` if the `sessionId` already exists.

- **`session:resolve-token`**
  - Input: `{ token: string }`
  - Output: `{ sessionId: string, workspaceRoot: string } | null`. Returns `null` (the literal) if the token is unknown OR if the session has been terminated.

- **`session:queue-work`**
  - Input: `{ sessionId: string, entry: InboxEntry }` where `InboxEntry = { type: 'user-message', payload: ChatMessage } | { type: 'cancel' }`.
  - Output: `{ cursor: number }` — the 0-based index the entry was stored at.
  - Errors: `PluginError(code: 'unknown-session')` if the session doesn't exist or has been terminated.

- **`session:claim-work`**
  - Input: `{ sessionId: string, cursor: number, timeoutMs: number }` — long-poll. If `entries[cursor]` exists, resolve immediately. Otherwise wait up to `timeoutMs` for a new entry.
  - Output: `{ type: 'user-message', payload: ChatMessage, cursor: number } | { type: 'cancel', cursor: number } | { type: 'timeout', cursor: number }`.
    - On `user-message` and `cancel`: the returned `cursor` is the NEXT cursor the caller should request (delivered-index + 1). This matches the `@ax/ipc-protocol` `SessionNextMessageResponseSchema` semantics: "server echoes the cursor the client should use on the NEXT request."
    - On `timeout`: the returned `cursor` echoes the input cursor (no advancement).
  - Errors: `PluginError(code: 'unknown-session')` if the session doesn't exist.

- **`session:terminate`**
  - Input: `{ sessionId: string }`
  - Output: `{}` (empty object). Idempotent — terminating an already-terminated or never-existed session is a no-op (no error). Documented.
  - Effect: Marks session terminated. `resolveToken` returns `null` thereafter. Any in-flight `claim-work` promises for this session resolve as `timeout` (they wake, see terminated, return the echo-cursor timeout variant). Any subsequent `queue-work` calls throw `unknown-session`.

### Internal shape

**`src/store.ts`** exports `createSessionStore()`:

```ts
export interface SessionRecord {
  readonly sessionId: string;
  readonly workspaceRoot: string;
  readonly token: string;
  terminated: boolean;
}

export interface SessionStore {
  create(sessionId: string, workspaceRoot: string): SessionRecord;
  resolveToken(token: string): { sessionId: string; workspaceRoot: string } | null;
  get(sessionId: string): SessionRecord | null;
  terminate(sessionId: string): void;
}
```

Two maps: `sessionId → SessionRecord` and `token → sessionId` (reverse lookup for O(1) resolve). `create` throws on duplicate `sessionId`. `terminate` sets the flag but does NOT delete — the reverse map still contains the terminated token, but `resolveToken` checks `terminated` and returns `null`. This lets forensic tooling still see the record; Week 7–9's postgres impl will match.

Token minting: `crypto.randomBytes(32).toString('base64url')` → regex-matchable `/^[A-Za-z0-9_-]{43}$/`.

**`src/inbox.ts`** exports `createInbox()`:

```ts
export interface Inbox {
  queue(sessionId: string, entry: InboxEntry): { cursor: number };
  claim(sessionId: string, cursor: number, timeoutMs: number): Promise<ClaimResult>;
  terminate(sessionId: string): void;  // wakes all pending claims on this session
}
```

Per-session state:
```ts
interface PerSession {
  entries: InboxEntry[];         // index === 0-based cursor
  waiters: Set<() => void>;      // wake callbacks
  terminated: boolean;
}
```

Lazy-init the per-session state on first `queue` or `claim` call (since the store is the authoritative existence check — the plugin layer calls `store.get()` before touching the inbox).

`claim(sessionId, cursor, timeoutMs)`:
1. If per-session state has an entry at `cursor`: return `{ type: entry.type, payload: entry.payload (if user-message), cursor: cursor + 1 }` immediately.
2. Otherwise, register a wake callback; set a timer for `timeoutMs`. Race them.
3. On wake: re-check `entries[cursor]`. If present, resolve as above. If not (waiter was woken by a terminate or a mistimed event), and the session is terminated, resolve `{ type: 'timeout', cursor }`. Else… this shouldn't happen, but to be safe, fall through to the timeout path.
4. On timer: resolve `{ type: 'timeout', cursor }`. Clean up the waiter.
5. On `terminate`: fire all waiters. Each waiter re-checks and either finds the entry (if it was queued just before) or sees terminated and resolves `timeout`.

Make sure: timer is cleared on wake; wake callback is removed from the set on timer. No leaking waiters. No leaking timers. Every promise resolves exactly once (use a `resolved` flag or a shared `resolve` reference).

**`src/types.ts`** exports the input/output types for each service hook plus `InboxEntry`:

```ts
import type { ChatMessage } from '@ax/core';

export type InboxEntry =
  | { type: 'user-message'; payload: ChatMessage }
  | { type: 'cancel' };

export type ClaimResult =
  | { type: 'user-message'; payload: ChatMessage; cursor: number }
  | { type: 'cancel'; cursor: number }
  | { type: 'timeout'; cursor: number };

export interface SessionCreateInput { sessionId: string; workspaceRoot: string; }
export interface SessionCreateOutput { sessionId: string; token: string; }

// ... etc. for the other four hooks
```

**`src/plugin.ts`** exports `createSessionInmemoryPlugin()` — constructs the store + inbox inside the factory closure, wires all five service hooks in `init()`. Each hook handler does input-shape sanity checks (e.g., `typeof sessionId === 'string'`) before calling through; throws structured `PluginError` on bad input. Keep it simple — no Zod needed for in-process hook payloads (Zod is for the IPC wire).

### Tests (TDD)

Split across three files:

**`__tests__/store.test.ts`** (~8 tests):
1. `create()` returns sessionId + 43-char base64url token.
2. Duplicate sessionId → `PluginError` code `duplicate-session`.
3. Two tokens in succession are distinct (statistical guard — token collisions would indicate broken randomness).
4. `resolveToken()` returns the session for a valid token.
5. `resolveToken()` returns `null` for an unknown token.
6. `resolveToken()` returns `null` for a terminated session.
7. `terminate()` is idempotent (calling twice doesn't throw).
8. `terminate()` on a non-existent session is a no-op.

**`__tests__/inbox.test.ts`** (~7 tests):
1. Queue then claim: claim returns the entry immediately with cursor = 1 (advanced).
2. Claim on empty inbox blocks then times out; returned cursor = input cursor (echoed).
3. Claim wakes when a new entry is queued during the wait (use a small `timeoutMs` like 500ms, queue after ~50ms, assert claim resolves before timeout).
4. Cursor advances monotonically across multiple queues (0, 1, 2).
5. Claim at a cursor beyond the end waits; queueing that entry wakes it.
6. Claim a `cancel` entry: returns `{ type: 'cancel', cursor: <next> }`.
7. `terminate()` during a blocked claim: claim resolves as `timeout` with echo cursor.

Use `vi.useFakeTimers()` with caution here — the real timer + `setImmediate` interaction can be subtle. If fake timers complicate the "wake during wait" test, use real timers with small `timeoutMs` (200–500 ms total test wall time).

**`__tests__/plugin.test.ts`** (~5 tests using `createTestHarness`):
1. Plugin registers all five hooks — bus has each via `bus.hasService()`.
2. End-to-end: `session:create` → `session:queue-work` (user-message) → `session:claim-work` round-trips.
3. `session:resolve-token` returns `null` after `session:terminate`.
4. `session:queue-work` on unknown session throws `PluginError` code `unknown-session`.
5. `session:claim-work` on unknown session throws `PluginError` code `unknown-session`.

Look at `packages/audit-log/src/__tests__/*.test.ts` and `packages/tool-dispatcher/src/__tests__/*.test.ts` for how `createTestHarness` is used with plugins. The harness's `plugins: [pluginFactory()]` option wires them through bootstrap.

## Commands

- `pnpm --filter @ax/session-inmemory test` — package tests
- `pnpm build` — whole-repo build
- `pnpm test` — whole-repo tests (should end at 236+ total tests)

## Self-review before reporting back

- **Completeness:** five hooks, correct I/O shapes, cursor semantics match the IPC-protocol contract (advance on delivery, echo on timeout).
